findings.title: Updater proceeds without verification when checksums.txt asset is absent
findings.description: downloadAndApply only verifies if checksumAsset is present (line 624). When release ships without checksums.txt, entire if-block skipped — bt downloads, extracts, runs new binary with --version (line 659), replaces running binary on rename success.
findings.evidence: if checksumAsset != nil at line 624 gates all verification. runCommand has zero timeout (line 718-722). No signature verification, no fallback to refusal, no warning.
findings.recommendation: Refuse to apply when checksumAsset is nil. Require --allow-unverified to override. Add timeout to runCommand. Long-term: sign with cosign/minisign.

findings.title: Arbitrary code execution via .bt/hooks.yaml in cloned repos
findings.description: Loader.Load() reads .bt/hooks.yaml from projectDir (default os.Getwd()) and passes Hook.Command into sh -c / cmd /C. --no-hooks flag default is false, so bt export executes hooks BY DEFAULT. Cloning untrusted repo + bt export = arbitrary shell execution under user's UID.
findings.evidence: Trust boundary is project working directory. No signature, allowlist, sandbox, prompt, or attestation. Standard 'git-config style' RCE vector.
findings.recommendation: Default to no-execute on first encounter. TOFU trust prompt. Hash + allow-record in ~/.bt/hook-trust.json. Invert flag default. Add bt hooks list/trust subcommands. Document in SECURITY.md.

findings.title: Data layer carries dead SourceTypeSQLite branch despite ADR-003 acceptance
findings.description: ADR-003 accepted 2026-04-25 with Option (b): collapse SourceType to Dolt|DoltGlobal|JSONLFallback. Implementation tracked in bt-05zt. At HEAD, SQLite discovery + reader still wired.
findings.evidence: discoverSQLiteSources at source.go:204 still in pipeline. LoadFromSource at load.go:172 still dispatches NewSQLiteReader. pages.go hand-rolls metadataPreferredSource.
findings.recommendation: Land bt-05zt Phase 1 (mechanical SQLite removal). Then Phase 2 (collapse enum). Fold pages.go's hand-rolled dispatch into canonical pipeline.

findings.location: AGENTS.md:146 vs internal/datasource/load.go:172, source.go:212, cmd/bt/pages.go:580-582, cmd/bt/root.go:247
findings.title: Project memory says SQLite is export-only; code wires SourceTypeSQLite into runtime
findings.description: AGENTS.md:146 states 'There is no SQLite at runtime.' Code contradicts: source.go discovers .beads/beads.db at PrioritySQLite=100; load.go dispatches SourceTypeSQLite; root.go lists SQLite fallback; pages.go instantiates SQLiteReader.
findings.evidence: Stale beads.db files from pre-v0.56.1 era get picked at PrioritySQLite=100 over JSONL=50, returning data that diverges from live Dolt.
findings.recommendation: Until ADR-003/bt-05zt lands, AGENTS.md:146 must be corrected. Right answer: ship bt-05zt Phase 1 now.

findings.title: pkg/loader/sprint.go is a stale-Dolt-assumption violation per AGENTS.md checklist
findings.description: AGENTS.md checklist explicitly: 'Does it assume .beads/sprints.jsonl exists?' Yet sprint.go:28 reads filepath.Join(repoPath, '.beads', 'sprints.jsonl'). cobra_robot.go wires bt robot sprint list/show, burndown, forecast --forecast-sprint.
findings.evidence: On Dolt-only beads installs (every install per AGENTS.md:72), file never exists; LoadSprints silently returns empty. Agents trying bt robot sprint list get empty results with no diagnostic.
findings.recommendation: Either gate subcommands to error with 'sprints not supported' message, or remove sprint subcommands from cobra registration. Doing nothing is worst option.

findings.title: AGENTS.md Key Directories lists internal/dolt/ and internal/models/ that do not exist
findings.description: AGENTS.md:88 declares internal/dolt/ and internal/models/. Actual: internal/ contains only datasource/, doltctl/, settings/. Dolt reader lives at internal/datasource/dolt.go; issue types at pkg/model/issue.go.
findings.evidence: Canonical onboarding doc points agents to two phantom packages and omits real ones.
findings.recommendation: Update AGENTS.md to: replace internal/dolt/ with internal/datasource/ + internal/doltctl/; replace internal/models/ with pkg/model/. Add pkg/view/ since it's load-bearing for robot output.

findings.title: N+1 query storm on every single-DB issue load (3 round-trips per issue)
findings.description: DoltReader.LoadIssuesFiltered runs issues SELECT then per-row loadLabels/loadDependencies/loadComments. Total queries = 1 + 3n. SetMaxOpenConns(5) means n>5 serialize, multiplying RTT by ~n/5.
findings.evidence: GlobalDoltReader has batched loadAllLabels/Dependencies/Comments — single-DB path doesn't. No benchmark covers.
findings.recommendation: Add batched loadAll* methods to DoltReader with WHERE issue_id IN (?,?,...). Mirror global reader's issueMap pattern. Add benchmark with n in {100, 500, 2000}.

findings.title: Git argument injection via unvalidated SHAs (no --end-of-options)
findings.description: SHAs from bead/cache flow to git subprocess as positional args without hex validation or --end-of-options. SHA beginning with -- treated as flag (--upload-pack=evil, --output=/tmp/x).
findings.evidence: incremental.go:200 sinceSHA appended unverified. stream.go:415 args = append(args, shas...). temporal.go:143 no validation. Prior STRIDE audit (260427) recommended validateSHA helper not implemented.
findings.recommendation: Add validateSHA requiring ^[0-9a-fA-F]{4,64}$. Insert --end-of-options before first positional ref. Audit checklist exists in docs/audits/security/260427-0039-stride-owasp-full-audit/recommendations.md.

findings.title: Phase 2 timeout fallback (uniform PageRank) silently persisted to disk cache
findings.description: On PageRank timer fire, code substitutes uniform 1/N PageRank, sets profile.PageRankTO=true, but execution continues. computePhase2 unconditionally calls putRobotDiskCachedStats; cache.go:850 only checks IsPhase2Ready (true even with degraded fallback).
findings.evidence: Transient slow run gets baked into on-disk cache; subsequent invocations load uniform-PageRank as if real.
findings.recommendation: Skip putRobotDiskCachedStats when ANY profile.*TO is true OR when status indicates timeout/panic. Alternatively, store timeout status in cache key.

findings.title: os.Exit short-circuits Dolt server cleanup, orphaning bd-spawned processes
findings.description: runRootTUI has no defer for serverState.StopIfOwned. ~13 os.Exit(1) calls in this function bypass deferred functions per Go spec. Successfully started Dolt server but exit via os.Exit (BQL parse error, recipe lookup, terminal detection) leaves bd-spawned Dolt server running.
findings.evidence: PID file at .beads/dolt-server.pid persists; next bt run sees still-live PID and attaches as non-owner, never cleans up.
findings.recommendation: Wrap runRootTUI with defer cleanup. Replace os.Exit pattern with cleanup-aware return. Register cleanup with signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM also bypass deferred cleanup).

findings.title: GlobalDoltReader.LoadIssues fails entire load if any single database errors
findings.description: LoadIssues builds one UNION ALL across all databases, executes once, returns first error. MySQL UNION ALL is all-or-nothing. Compare LoadIssuesAsOf which queries each DB individually with per-DB error accumulation.
findings.evidence: User with 12 working + 1 broken project sees zero issues live but full history with --as-of — opposite of expectation.
findings.recommendation: Refactor LoadIssues to mirror LoadIssuesAsOf's per-DB loop. Log+skip per-DB errors, succeed if >=1 DB loaded. Expose failed databases in result so UI can surface degraded state.

findings.title: Temporal correlator runs git subprocesses sequentially per bead with nested fanout
findings.description: ExtractAllTemporalCorrelations iterates m beads, each FindCommitsInWindow spawns git log; per-commit calls touchesBeadsFile (git show) + ExtractCoCommittedFiles (git). For m=100, k=10 avg: ~3000 sequential git subprocesses on critical path.
findings.evidence: BatchFileStatsExtractor at stream.go:364 already uses git log --no-walk for batched fetch — built but not used here.
findings.recommendation: (a) Parallelize outer loop with errgroup capped at runtime.NumCPU(). (b) Replace per-commit git calls with BatchFileStatsExtractor.ExtractBatch(shas) — infrastructure exists.

findings.title: Robot output schemas exist for only 4 types; ~26 subcommands have no regression net
findings.description: AGENTS.md:144 promises 'deterministic JSON' but pkg/view/schemas/ contains only compact_issue.v1, pair_record.v{1,2}, portfolio_record.v1, ref_record.v{1,2}. cobra_robot.go registers ~30 subcommands. bt robot schema generates schemas at runtime — circular validation. Zero golden snapshot files under cmd/bt/.
findings.evidence: Most agent-facing surface in codebase has no regression net for ~26/30 subcommands. bt-ah53 P2→P1 bump (2026-04-28) acknowledged but OPEN.
findings.recommendation: Capture golden snapshots of every bt robot <subcmd> --shape={full,compact} against fixed fixtures (helpers exist in robot_all_subcommands_test.go). Diff in go test.

findings.title: Self-update has no GPG/cosign signature verification
findings.description: Flow: HTTPS download -> checksums.txt download -> SHA256 verify -> extract -> run new binary with --version -> install. Gap: checksums.txt from same release, not signature-verified. Compromise of GitHub release replaces both bt_*.tar.gz AND checksums.txt.
findings.recommendation: Sign artifacts with cosign keyless (Sigstore). Verify signature before extraction. Interim: GPG checksums.txt.sig with embedded pubkey. Stop running new binary with --version for 'verification'.

findings.title: pkg/loader/sprint.go reads file beads never produces
findings.description: Complete user-facing feature (loader, type, TUI mode, robot subcommand) pretending to be domain primitive but pointing at empty filesystem location.
findings.recommendation: bt-z5jj's call. Either retire the sprint feature entirely or rebuild against Dolt. Current limbo is worst-of-both-worlds.

findings.title: AGENTS.md says 15 files; actual is 19 across 5 directories
findings.description: AGENTS.md:158 says filename hardcoded in pkg/agents/ (15 files). Actual: 19 Go files across pkg/agents/, pkg/ui/, pkg/baseline/, cmd/bt/, tests/e2e/.
findings.recommendation: Update AGENTS.md to correct count. Better: factor into single agents.AgentsFileName constant.

findings.title: cobra_robot.go is a 1,301-line registration god-file holding 50+ subcommand definitions
findings.recommendation: Split BY COMMAND GROUP (not per subcommand to avoid init() spaghetti). Each group (search, history, sprint, labels, files, correlation, baseline) gets own file.

findings.title: Workspace/repo loading split across 4 packages with no clear ownership boundary
findings.recommendation: Use ADR-003 Phase 2 as forcing function. After SourceType collapse, audit every 'load issues' callsite and route through single entry point. pages.go's metadataPreferredSource disappears into datasource.

findings.title: Stale-lock takeover races on Windows via unguarded remove+rename window
findings.description: Windows fallback uses Remove + Rename non-atomic. Attacker with .beads/ write can plant symlink between operations — subsequent Rename and Release follow link.
findings.recommendation: On Windows use LockFileEx with FILE_FLAG_OPEN_REPARSE_POINT or os.Lstat refuse if symlink. Add TOCTOU guard. Hold lockFile handle for lifetime.

findings.title: Live-reload SSE endpoint sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * with no CSRF check
findings.description: Cross-origin pages can EventSource the localhost preview server. DNS-rebinding bypass leaks bundle_path (full FS path including username).
findings.recommendation: Drop CORS:*. Add Origin header check. Add Host header check (DNS-rebinding defense). Stop returning absolute bundle path.

findings.title: Stdout-regex parsing of bd dolt start fails open via attacker-controlled .beads files
findings.description: Attacker plants .beads/dolt-server.{pid,port} pointing at attacker port speaking MySQL protocol. Combined with non-matching upstream stdout, bt attaches and issues SQL (root no-password) against attacker.
findings.recommendation: After connect, send SELECT @@version requiring Dolt version. Validate lookPath('bd') outside cwd. Treat parse failure as hard error.

findings.title: BatchFileStatsExtractor cache is unbounded
findings.recommendation: Mirror incrementalGraphStatsCache pattern: max-size cap + LRU eviction (~1000 entries).

findings.title: Phase 2 metric timeouts leak goroutines
findings.description: Goroutine continues running gonum to completion after timeout, holding entire graph reference. Repeated re-analysis piles up abandoned goroutines.
findings.recommendation: Replace timer with context.WithTimeout. Have inner algorithm honor cancellation (computePageRank loop can check ctx.Err()). Hard cap on Phase-2 goroutine concurrency.

findings.title: countBlockedBy is O(n^2*d) inside BFS loop
findings.recommendation: Pre-compute blockedByCount once at Analyzer construction (or reuse stats.InDegree). Memoize repeated GetOpenBlockers calls.

findings.title: Live-reload debouncer drops trailing event in write burst (wrong direction)
findings.description: Leading-edge skip pattern fires on FIRST event in burst, drops subsequent. Browser reloads against partially-flushed assets.
findings.recommendation: Replace with trailing-edge Debouncer from pkg/watcher/debouncer.go. Add 100ms grace delay.

findings.title: Panicking Phase 2 metric goroutines silently fall through to timeout fallback
findings.description: Empty recover() body in each goroutine. Real algorithmic bugs hidden behind benign-looking timeout.
findings.recommendation: Send sentinel from recover (panicCh in parent select). Log debug.Stack(). Mark profile.*Panic=true.

findings.title: All git subprocess invocations run without context/timeout
findings.recommendation: Pass through context.Context to all loader/correlation entry points. Use exec.CommandContext with per-call timeout. Add BT_GIT_TIMEOUT env var.

findings.title: doltctl regex parse failure falls back to .beads PID/port files that may not exist
findings.recommendation: Coordinate upstream beads for bd dolt start --json or bd dolt status. Until then, prefer reading pid/port files (intentional) and use regex as backup.

findings.title: Tutorial and TUI render code still references bv-* (beads_viewer-era) bead IDs
findings.recommendation: Close bt-t82t by sweeping bv-* refs in non-archive code paths. Quickest sweep: pkg/ui/ and pkg/baseline/ only.

findings.title: pkg/ui Model is a god object: 50+ flat fields across 16 files, 165 method receivers
findings.recommendation: Continue bt-oim6/bt-if3w.1 component-extraction pattern. Group: AlertsModel, SemanticSearchState, LabelDashboardState, TimeTravelState. Target: ~10 field groups + router.

findings.title: graph.go is 2,880 LOC with 111 functions: single file owning entire graph-analysis surface
findings.recommendation: Split per-algorithm: pagerank.go, eigenvector.go, kcore.go. Move rank helpers to ranks.go. Move Phase 2 orchestration to phase2.go. Delete deprecated map-copy methods.

findings.title: Stale lock takeover races silently when multiple bt instances start simultaneously
findings.recommendation: Hold lock file open with OS-level advisory lock (flock POSIX, LockFileEx Windows). Add takeover-race regression test on Windows.

findings.title: Release pipeline workflow_dispatch defaults snapshot=true; manual trigger cannot cut real release
findings.recommendation: Either flip snapshot:false default (with separate snapshot-test workflow), or document the trap in release runbook. Update MEMORY.md.

findings.title: UNION ALL across all databases scales linearly with workspace count and never times out individual segments
findings.recommendation: Per-DB queries in parallel errgroup with per-DB context.WithTimeout, merge in Go. Or add readTimeout to DSN.

findings.title: Single-quote escaping in cross-database UNION ALL queries depends on dbName validation
findings.recommendation: Use ? placeholders for dbName in string-literal positions. Identifier positions keep backtickQuote but assert in EnumerateDatabases. SET sql_mode='NO_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES,ANSI_QUOTES' on connection open.

findings.title: Supply-chain risk: pseudo-version dependency on personal author's repo without vendor integrity attestation
findings.recommendation: Pin Dicklesworthstone/toon-go to tagged release or vendor frozen copy under pkg/toon/. CI step: go mod verify + go mod vendor diff check. Add govulncheck. Document in SECURITY.md.

findings.title: cmd/bt directly imports pkg/ui, coupling CLI binary to entire 38.9k-LOC TUI surface
findings.description: Every bt robot <subcmd> invocation links pkg/ui closure (charm.land/* + clipboard).
findings.recommendation: Either accept single-binary in ADR or wire pkg/ui behind tui build tag with bt-min variant.

findings.title: Legacy bv- sigils embedded in 98 first-party files signal incomplete identity migration
findings.recommendation: Reframe bt-t82t to include source-code sigil sweep. Translate, drop, or prefix with 'originally tracked in bv-XXXX, see archive'.

hypotheses_summary: See hypothesis-queue.md for 35 testable hypotheses + 1 minority, organized by cluster for chain-handoff.
non_code_hypotheses_from_devils_advocate: Phase 2 async-with-timeout pattern is wrong abstraction for interactive TUI; alternative is synchronous bounded-time computation with explicit progress callbacks (round 1)
Doc-drift cluster suggests AGENTS.md/MEMORY.md need a CI grep gate that verifies path/count claims against current state (round 2)
